name: Lewis Transportation LLC
recent_news: No recent news located; minimal online footprint. Listed only in directory aggregators as a 'Bus Charter and Rental' business at 2929 Boarman Ave, Baltimore.
website: unverified (lewistransportinc.com in source data appears to belong to a separate Baltimore freight/auto-transport carrier owned by Rodney Lewis; the Boarman Ave entity has no confirmed standalone website)
fit_notes: Very small (~16 employees, ~$1.2M rev) Baltimore charter/bus operator with a thin web presence. Hard to validate as a school-bus contractor; would require direct outreach to confirm whether it actually runs school routes for Baltimore City/County districts. Low priority until basic operating profile is verified.

name: Voigt's Bus Service
districts_or_customers: various MN districts via 4 facilities (Cold Spring, Minneapolis, St. Augusta, St. Cloud) and Waconia HQ
recent_news: No M&A activity identified; remains independent third-generation family business operating 100+ buses across four MN locations.
fit_notes: Attractive MN tuck-in: 3rd-generation family ownership (succession optionality), 100+ buses, anchor Anoka-Hennepin contract, diversified school/charter/motorcoach mix, 4 facilities offering geographic density across central MN. Employee count in source (18) is materially understated vs. actual scale; revenue likely well above the $1.1M listed.

name: Harlow's School Bus Service Inc-MT
owner_or_parent: Harlow's Bus Service Inc. (Bismarck, ND) - Hageness family
districts_or_customers: Libby MT-area school districts; parent operates across ID/MT/ND/SD/WA/NE
recent_news: No M&A activity identified; remains family-owned multi-state platform led by three Hageness brothers (2nd generation). Libby, MT location is a regional branch of the broader Harlow's Bus Service Inc. (Bismarck, ND HQ).
fit_notes: MT 'company' is a branch of a larger 2nd-gen family-owned regional school bus + motorcoach + bus-sales platform headquartered in Bismarck, ND covering 6 states. Cannot acquire just the Libby branch in isolation; would have to approach the parent (Hageness family). Three active sibling owners with 25+ years signals lower near-term succession urgency, but the multi-state footprint and diversified revenue (school bus + Trailways charter + Harlow's truck/bus dealership) make the platform itself an interesting larger-than-tuck-in target if family ever transacts.

name: Easton Coach Co
owner_or_parent: Beacon Mobility Corp. (Audax Private Equity portfolio company)
recent_news: Acquired by Van Pool Transportation LLC (Beacon Mobility / Audax PE) effective Sept 28, 2021 per STB Federal Register notice; rebranded as a Beacon Mobility partner company. Continues to operate under same name across ~18-20 PA/NJ locations; subsequent Beacon add-ons (Cedar Bus, Easton Bus Service, Butler's Bus, WE Transport, etc.) tucked under same platform. Beacon Mobility itself reportedly in a sale process (ION/Mergermarket 2024-25).
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in candidate. Owned since 2021 by Beacon Mobility (Audax PE) - a direct strategic competitor to Summit. With ~1,300 vehicles and 2,000 employees concentrated in PA/NJ paratransit & motorcoach, Easton Coach is a Beacon platform asset, not an independent target. Remove from tuck-in funnel; monitor only in context of any Beacon Mobility portfolio carve-out or future Beacon sale process.

name: Transportation Unlimited Inc
principals: Carlton Melone (listed as Owner in source data)
service_types: special-needs role per source data but not confirmed on website
recent_news: No M&A or news located. Website (tuibusservice.com) presents as a Dallas-TX-area charter operator (214 area code) despite D&B listing of Theodore, AL HQ; possible mismatch between corporate registration and operating brand/territory. FMCSA USDOT 2178728 record is INACTIVE in SAFER, raising questions about current active operating authority.
website: https://www.tuibusservice.com (Texas/Dallas branded; relationship to Theodore, AL listing unclear)
fit_notes: Multiple red flags: inactive FMCSA record under one USDOT, geographic mismatch between AL HQ and TX-facing website/phone, no confirmed school district contracts, and a charter-coach orientation rather than yellow-bus school operations. Likely a small charter operator using a 'special-needs' tag loosely; not a credible school-bus tuck-in until operating status, ownership, and actual school/SPED contracts can be verified by direct outreach.
